summary:
FNAC may miss follicular carcinomas due to overlapping cell morphology; further testing such as immunostains is often required for definitive diagnosis. Other cancers can typically still be diagnosed adequately through FNAC without needing more invasive procedures unless there’s significant uncertainty after a single procedure.
 The summary focuses on two main aspects regarding why fine-needle aspirate cytology isn't sufficient for diagnosing certain types of tumors specifically mentioning that it lacks accuracy concerning follicular carcinomas while remaining effective otherwise except perhaps requiring confirmation tests depending upon context provided within specific cases encountered clinically which vary widely Other cancers can typically still be diagnosed adequately through FNAC without needing more invasive procedures unless there’s significant uncertainty after a single procedure.
